extraction_metadata.pir06_threshold_status: NOT CROSSED — no formal PN-MT removal notice for Bersatu detected across 5 cycles (5,513 titles scanned: 1151+1174+1164+1151+1160). Bersatu operational split FULL TEXT confirmed (24 own-logo seats; PAS severed ties June 8). CRITICAL precursor cluster now 5: (1) Kiandee 'asas kukuh' removal call, (2) Kiandee quorum-question ESCALATION (NEW this build), (3) 'toxic PN' claim-counterclaim (FULL TEXT FMT), (4) Ridzuan Ahmad quits Bersatu, (5) Muhyiddin 'new coalition after state election' PN-exit hint (FULL TEXT FMT — strongest pre-threshold signal). Watch next cycle for Bersatu Supreme Council response to quorum question + post-NS-PRN formalisation.
collection_limitations_honest: Malaysiakini paywall: mkini articles (Loke Malay votes 780073, Zan Azlee 780063, AI chatbot 780066, Johor exco 780048) captured as previews (91-600c) only; full body paywalled

gnews protobuf resolution via curl: CONFIRMED INFEASIBLE across 4 cycles — intermediate is 588KB JS SPA, protobuf base64 decodes to encrypted tokens only. 10 items this build saved as headline-intelligence (title+pubdate+source). Kiandee-quorum NST article is headline-intel only — full text requires NST feed (rotated past top-50) or JS-render

NST WordPress feed: only latest 50 items — older high-value NST articles (Asyraf Wajdi BN-PN stability, Hadi PN-assist-BN, Bersatu goes solo, Loke slams traitors, Linggi risky) fell outside top-50 window → headline-intelligence only
The Star + Malay Mail search/listing: JS-rendered; Melaka DAP + Fahmi articles captured as headline intelligence only

SOLVED this build: FMT (curl-friendly RSS+direct) + NST WordPress feed + Awani direct-fetch (berita-politik slug) now provide reliable full-text paths — substantially closes prior 'JS-render blocker' gap for three major sources
new_full_text_sources_this_build: FMT RSS (content:encoded) — 19 full-text articles (NEW SOURCE, largest single-day gain)
NST WordPress feed (content:encoded) — 7 full-text articles (Tok Mat, Anwar resign, Hadi unity, Anwar Ali Baba, Johari Johor-formula, NS police, Melaka shophouse)
Astro Awani direct (berita-politik slug) — 3 full-text (Klawang cousins N.28 T1, Pengundi kempen matang, Teka-teki 11 kerusi PN candidate list)
mkini direct — 1 paywalled preview (Zan Azlee indicator v2, 513c)
gnews headline-intel — 10 items (Kiandee quorum, PAS-may-leave analysts, Anthony-Loké-Awani-Intl, PH-lineup-tomorrow, Newswav x3, Star x1, Malay Mail dup)

entities.source_url: https://www.nst.com.my (gnews: Hadi dismisses Muhyiddin's 'toxic PN' claim, points finger at Bersatu)
entities.context: Bersatu president; PN chairman. PIR-06 CRITICAL figure — now FULL TEXT via FMT ('Bersatu must survive as lone opposition', 18 Jul 16:52 MYT): frames Bersatu as 'country's only credible opposition'; PN's BN arrangement means PN 'indirectly supports government.' FMT 19 Jul 09:00 MYT: 'Muhyiddin said the party will form a new coalition after the state election, hinting at a PN exit' — STRONGEST pre-threshold PIR-06 signal. Also accuses Umno/BN of 'charade to deceive the Malays' re ummah/Malay unity; claims Bersatu 'free of all this charade' + committed to anti-kleptocracy/1MDB founding struggle. Original: subject of 'toxic PN' claim (dismissed by Hadi); 'Bersatu to use own logo for NS polls' (24 seats).

entities.source_url: https://www.nst.com.my (gnews: Hadi dismisses Muhyiddin's 'toxic PN' claim)
entities.context: PAS president. PIR-06 CRITICAL — now FULL TEXT via FMT ('Hadi denies PAS made PN toxic, blames Bersatu instead', 18 Jul 12:48 MYT): 'rift caused by Bersatu's misconduct within PN'; says Bersatu new-coalition plan 'up to his former ally'; wants to EXPAND BN-PN cooperation NATIONWIDE. NST full text (19 Jul 14:03 MYT): calls for Malay-Muslim unity with 'non-extreme' partners incl MCA, MIC, DHPP; reject 'unnatural Malay groups with DAP.' FMT BM (19 Jul 09:30): 'Bukan retorik' — PN-BN ties 'lebih daripada berkahwin' (more than marriage). Jempol nomination (Serting, Palong, Jeram Padang, Bahau). PAS severed political ties with Bersatu June 8.

entities.source_url: https://www.nst.com.my (gnews: PN has grounds to remove Bersatu, says Kiandee)
entities.context: Bersatu VP. PIR-06 CRITICAL precursor. ORIGINAL: 'PN has grounds (asas kukuh) to remove Bersatu' (call, not formal notice). NEW THIS CYCLE (NST headline-intel, 19 Jul): ESCALATION — 'Kiandee asks Muhyiddin if Bersatu Supreme Council still has quorum' — from 'grounds to remove' to 'is the decision-making body even functional?' (implying mass exits hollowed it out). Director-flagged 'pecat'-type signal; threshold still NOT crossed.

entities.source_url: https://www.nst.com.my (gnews: Former Gemas assemblyman Ridzuan Ahmad quits Bersatu [WATCH])
entities.context: CRITICAL PIR-06 'keluar' signal confirmed: former Gemas incumbent, resigned as Bersatu division chief and QUIT Bersatu ahead of NS polls; now contesting Gemas seat under Parti Wawasan Negara. Confirmed via Malay Mail lineup + The Edge headline + NST headline. Director-flagged 'keluar' entity.

entities.source_url: https://www.nst.com.my (gnews: Hadi dismisses Muhyiddin's 'toxic PN' claim, points finger at Bersatu)
entities.context: CRITICAL director-flagged 'toxic PN' entity — now FULL TEXT confirmed via FMT (18 Jul 12:48 MYT). Attributed to Muhyiddin (PAS making PN toxic), DENIED by Hadi who counter-accuses Bersatu ('rift caused by Bersatu's misconduct within PN'). Claim/counter-claim exchange is the strongest internal-rupture discourse signal. Muhyiddin had accused PAS of 'baseless allegations and defamatory claims'; cited Perlis political crisis (MB post PAS→Bersatu).

PIR-06 Tier-4 seat AND PIR-07 highest-priority battleground. 3-cornered: Nor Azman Mohamad (PH, Aminuddin's pol-sec) vs Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz (Bersatu info chief) vs Razali Abu Samah (Wawasan). Tests PH without MB on ballot. 'Fleeing Sikamat' narrative carrier.

PIR-06 Tier-4 seat AND PIR-07 highest-priority battleground. CANDIDATE CONFIRMED via Awani teka-teki (16 Jul 23:12): PN candidate = Mohamad Rafie Ab Malek (director-flagged 'Rafie'). Director-flagged PH candidate Nazri Kassim. Day-1 messaging war: PH 'defector' vs PN 'experienced rep' (carryover).

PIR-07 highest-priority battleground (director list) — candidate detail NOW RECOVERED via Awani full text (Day-1 campaign, 19 Jul 12:11 MYT). 3-cornered: PH incumbent Datuk Bakri Sawir vs PN's Danni Rais (Bakri's COUSIN) vs Bersatu's Muhammad Adib Musa. 13,355 registered voters. Day-1: both cousins campaigned at Pasar Minggu Kuala Klawang (Jelebu) simultaneously — met peacefully, joked, no provocation. Bakri took down flags after Majlis Daerah Jelebu warning. Mature-campaign framing. HIGHEST PIR-07 value this cycle.

PIR-07 highest-priority battleground (director list). 3-cornered MB battle: Aminuddin Harun (PH, moved from Sikamat) vs Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli (BN, strong opponent) vs Datuk Zamri Md Said (Bersatu). 'Not taking for granted' + 'fleeing Sikamat' narratives both anchored here.
Director-flagged PIR-07 Faizal Ramli entity. BN's strong opponent to MB Aminuddin in Linggi (N.32). Carrier of 'Linggi stronghold cannot be taken for granted' framing.

PIR-07 highest-priority battleground (director list) — candidate detail NOW RECOVERED via FMT Tok Mat article (18 Jul). Straight fight: BN's Zaifulbahri Idris (since 2008; 2023 maj 4,335) vs PH's Danish Nazran Murad. (Prior cycle: candidate detail not recovered — now resolved.)

PRIORITY director-flagged PIR-16 viral-content amplifier. Businessperson in PN camp who called Harapan supporters 'barking dogs'; says he referred to those who provoked and insulted him. Article /news/780042 (mkini) — full body NOT fetched this cycle; entity derived from mkini homepage headline+description. Full name 'Albert Tei' per director PIR-16 list.

PRIORITY director-flagged PIR-16 viral-content amplifier narrative. PN-camp businessperson Albert Tei labelled Harapan supporters 'barking dogs'. High viral-potential insult narrative entering Day-1 campaign.

PRIORITY director-flagged PIR-16 narrative. 'Company gave Bersatu RM1m for unknown reasons, witness tells court in Muhyiddin's graft trial' — Nepturis managing director testified payment made on instruction of company owner. Active trial narrative framing Bersatu/Muhyiddin credibility.

PRIORITY director-flagged PIR-16 narrative. DAP QUIT Melaka state govt over bill allowing appointed/nominated assemblymen; 4 DAP reps + 1 Amanah (Adly) moved to opposition bloc. DOMINANT narrative directly framing DAP acceptance in NS PRN Malay seats. Anwar urged postpone; Melaka CM said 'abrupt exit shuts door on negotiations'; Perak unity ties unaffected.

PRIORITY director-flagged PIR-16 'Loke (DAP)' key figure. DAP sec-gen / Transport Minister / Seremban MP. Defends Chennah (PH). Day-1: urged traffic-rule compliance (helmet use, no standing on 4x4s) after PH candidates met visitors at Pasar Besar Seremban. PIR-16 narratives attached: leadership-referendum framing, 'penyaluran dana' adat controversy, DAP-not-quitting-Harapan, 'campaign of hope'.

PRIORITY director-flagged PIR-16 narrative. NS adat/istana (royal) crisis surfaced as PRN campaign issue. Tok Mat: 'keep adat out of polls campaign' (mkini SNAPSHOT 18 Jul 14:06). Aminuddin criticised for failing to handle adat crisis; seat-move linked to adat dispute (Utusan rencana). Loke 'penyaluran dana' controversy with Balai Undang Luak Sungai Ujong. Anwar: 'Don't campaign on NS ruler crisis'. Rembau chieftain sacked 3 nobles; court injunction.

PRIORITY director-flagged PIR-16 narrative — now FULL TEXT corroborated. Kosmo: 'memaksimumkan pengundian Melayu.' NEW: Hadi (NST 14:03 MYT) 'Malay-Muslim unity with non-extreme partners' + reject 'unnatural Malay groups with DAP.' FMT akar umbi (19 Jul): grassroots 'penyatuan ummah' framing — 'Penyatuan ummah inilah yang diharapkan oleh akar umbi Umno.' Muhyiddin counters: 'charade to deceive the Malays.' Tok Mat: 'safeguard the future of race and religion.'

PRIORITY director-flagged PIR-16 narrative — now FULL TEXT confirmed via FMT (Jalaluddin Alias, 18 Jul): 'BN to decide NS MB candidate only if it wins polls'; 'several leaders capable of helming the incoming administration'; final decision rests with coalition leadership. Jalaluddin among those touted as potential MB. Aminuddin (outgoing MB) moved to Linggi; if PH loses, MB question opens. Utusan rencana frames Aminuddin seat-move + adat-crisis as MB-credibility referendum.

PRIORITY director-flagged PIR-16 narrative — now FULL TEXT via FMT Muhyiddin (18 Jul 16:52 MYT): 'Bersatu must survive as lone opposition'; 'no longer a credible party to check and balance government policies'; PN's BN arrangement means PN 'indirectly supports the government.' Bersatu exit-from-PN question (mkini SNAPSHOT 18 Jul 18:00) + 24 own-logo seats reframe opposition bloc identity. Muhyiddin says Bersatu 'must survive' to fill the 'huge vacuum.'

PRIORITY director-flagged PIR-16 narrative (per list). 'Easy majority' framing — watch for emergence in campaign coverage; not yet directly quoted in this cycle's recovered text but listed as PIR-16 narrative to monitor.

entities.entity: Harapan Youth demands BN ministers quit
entities.context: PRIORITY viral-content amplifier. AMH (Angkatan Muda Harapan) demands BN ministers/deputy ministers quit Cabinet over PN alliance — 'BN is ungrateful despite trust and positions given by PM.' mkini top story + Astro Awani. Day-1 amplification of BN-PN cooperation backlash.

PRIORITY viral-content amplifier. mkini (Zarrah Morden): 'DAP revives anti-BN campaign ahead of NS polls — leaders bring up 1MDB scandal, ruler crisis, and political manoeuvres.' Direct PIR-16 narrative activation tying NS campaign to national anti-BN themes.

Perikatan Nasional. In NS: PN = PAS+Gerakan+Wawasan+MIPP (11 candidates) under BN-PN understanding. Bersatu broke consensus by going solo (24 own-logo seats). PN-MT (Majlis Tertinggi) has NOT issued formal removal notice for Bersatu.

Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia. Operational split FULL TEXT confirmed: 24 own-logo seats, broke PN state consensus. PAS severed political ties June 8 (FMT) — both remain in PN. Subject of Kiandee removal call + quorum question + 'toxic PN' claim-counterclaim. Ridzuan Ahmad quit. Analysts (FMT full text): Lau Zhe Wei (IIUM) advises quit PN + take Gerakan/MIPP; Azeem Fazwan (USM) expects wipeout; Azmil Tayeb (USM) — 'all people see is infighting.'
PN Supreme Council — director PIR-06 watch entity. Has NOT convened/issued formal removal notice for Bersatu. Threshold for formal removal NOT crossed across 3,489 titles scanned.
Director PIR-06 watch entity (coalition operational arrangement). PAS president Hadi Awang is the active carrier of 'brotherhood/friendship' + 'toxic PN' counter-claim framing.
Director PIR-06 watch entity. Muhyiddin-led. Decision to go solo under own logo in NS (24 seats) breaks PN state consensus — operational split decision-maker.
Director PIR-06 watch entity. Bersatu sec-gen / PN sec-gen. Not directly quoted in this cycle's recovered full text; carried as watch figure per director list.
Tan Sri Annuar Musa — director PIR-06 watch entity. Not directly quoted in this cycle's recovered full text; carried as watch figure per director list.

entities.source_url: https://www.nst.com.my (gnews: PN may continue BN cooperation if Negri Sembilan results prove positive, says Samsuri)
entities.context: Director PIR-06 'Samsuri' entity (Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar, PAS VP / Terengganu MB). States PN may continue BN cooperation beyond NS if results prove positive — forward-looking coalition arrangement signal.

entities.entity: Bersatu goes solo under own logo
entities.source_url: https://www.malaysiakini.com/news/779996 + https://www.nst.com.my (gnews: Bersatu goes solo in Negri Sembilan polls, PN says move expected)
entities.context: PIR-06 dominant operational-split narrative — now FULL TEXT confirmed across FMT (x4), NST, Awani. Muhyiddin: Bersatu to use own logo for NS polls. 24 own-logo seats. Breaks PN state consensus. PAS severed political ties with Bersatu June 8 (FMT); both remain in PN. PN says move 'expected.' Strongest operational-split confirmation; NOT a formal expulsion.

entities.source_url: https://www.nst.com.my (gnews: Negri Sembilan polls: Bersatu at a crossroads amid BN-PN cooperation [WATCH])
entities.context: PIR-06 narrative. NST frames Bersatu as 'at a crossroads' amid BN-PN cooperation — disarray/strategic-choice framing.
PIR-06+PIR-16 narrative. mkini SNAPSHOT (18 Jul 18:00 MYT) amplifies 'Bersatu exit from PN imminent?' as live rhetorical question. Headline itself is the signal (body paywalled).

PIR-16 DOMINANT official-framing narrative — now FULL TEXT corroborated across 3 sources. Wee Ka Siong (MCA, FMT 18 Jul): 'cooperation does not amount to an alliance... each party upholds ideological principles... checks and balances.' Mohd Isam Mohd Isa (Tampin Umno, NST 19 Jul 11:59): 'merely an understanding to face the election, not a political merger'; BN-MCA/MIC relationships 'remain intact.' Zahid: 'merely an understanding, no agreements.' Hadi: 'lebih daripada berkahwin' but formal ties 'dinilai dari semasa ke semasa.'

PIR-16 narrative. MCA Youth sec-gen Saw Yee Fung told to 'stay out of NS polls' over PN-ally disagreement; MCA sec-gen Chong Sin Woon WhatsApp: 'you don't have to come.' Shows BN-PN cooperation causing internal MCA friction.

MCA Youth sec-gen. Criticised BN's decision to cooperate with PN; instructed to skip NS campaign. Facebook post with screenshot of WhatsApp from Chong Sin Woon: 'you don't have to come.' Questions if party no longer tolerates dissenting views.
MCA secretary-general. Sent WhatsApp message 'you don't have to come' to Saw Yee Fung re NS campaign — carrier of MCA internal dissent narrative.
Datuk Dr Wee Ka Siong, MCA president. Carrier of 'BN-PN pact NOT a merger, MCA principles intact' dominant PIR-16 official framing (18 Jul 14:47 MYT).
PIR-07+16 narrative. NS Police: 1 election-offense report (verbal dispute) from nomination day; no investigation paper; 19 ceramah permits ALL APPROVED. Loke campaign Day-1 at Pasar Besar Seremban.

PIR-09+16 narrative. Tamim Dahri Abd Razak (self-proclaimed land activist) surrendered at Port Dickson police HQ 12:45pm; on the run from court charges linked to religious provocation; hopes to campaign for NS independent candidate. Red notice applied for (Anwar statement).

entities.source_url: https://www.utusan.com.my/nasional/2026/07/felda-mesti-terus-angkat-martabat-peneroka-anwar/ + https://www.nst.com.my (gnews: Anwar: Give PH a stronger mandate to ensure continuity)
entities.context: PM. 'Give PH a stronger mandate to ensure continuity' (NST). Urged Melaka DAP to postpone quit-state-govt decision; says 'not briefed' on DAP reps quitting. 'Don't campaign on NS ruler crisis; red notice applied for Tamim' (mkini). Felda statement (Utusan). Zahid to meet Anwar on NS govt administration issue.

BN bigwig retaining Pertang seat (Kosmo). Among 'tokoh besar kem' keeping position in nomination lineup.

entities.context: Fahmi (Pakatan). 'Will let Tok Min's track record do the talking' — anchor for Aminuddin (Tok Min = Aminuddin Harun) re-election messaging. Article full-text NOT recovered (Malay Mail homepage extraction failed).
NS Police Chief. Confirmed 1 election-offense report (verbal dispute, no investigation paper) + 19 ceramah permits ALL APPROVED. Cited re Tamim surrender (PD police HQ 12:45pm).

Self-proclaimed land activist. Surrendered at PD police HQ 12:45pm (18 Jul); on the run from court charges linked to religious provocation; hopes to campaign for NS independent candidate. Red notice applied for (Anwar statement, mkini gnews). PIR-09+16 independent-credibility narrative carrier.

Election Commission (SPR) chairman. Confirmed 103 candidates / 36 seats; 11 straight, 21 three-cornered, 2 four-cornered, 2 five-cornered; 94 men / 9 women; oldest Abd Latif A Tambi (70, Gemencheh), youngest Leevineshwaraan Murugan (23, Sri Tanjung).
NS adat institution. Disputed a 'penyaluran dana' entity that Loke/DAP were alleged to have funded; Loke denies DAP interfering in adat/royal institution. PIR-16 adat-dispute narrative carrier.
NS royal figure (adat/royal crisis). Referenced in prior-cycle entity build; adat-dispute narrative peripheral figure.

mkini COMMENT 'Oh, so the state elections are an indicator?' (19 Jul 09:49 MYT). Argues state elections as national-indicator framing; 'we will probably see another hung parliament after next GE.' PIR-16 indicator narrative carrier.
mkini KINIGUIDE author: 'Shifting alliances as battle royale for Negeri Sembilan begins' — primer on NS alliance landscape. Also authored 'From PN camp, Albert calls Harapan supporters barking dogs'.
mkini COMMENT 'Can the derhaka card defeat BN?' — PIR-16 derhaka/adat narrative carrier; argues 'obvious who defied the state monarch' in NS.
mkini COMMENT 'Malaysia's culture of khianat and the ballot box' — PIR-16 betrayal/loyalty narrative carrier.
mkini author: 'DAP revives anti-BN campaign ahead of NS polls' (1MDB, ruler crisis, political manoeuvres).
mkini author: 'N Sembilan polls: Bersatu to contest under own flag, avoids BN, Harapan strongholds' — PIR-06 operational-split narrative.
mkini co-author (with Hariz Mohd) of 'Albert calls Harapan supporters barking dogs' article.
mkini author: 'Company gave Bersatu RM1m for unknown reasons, witness tells court in Muhyiddin's graft trial' — PIR-16 Muhyiddin graft trial narrative.
mkini author: PSM 'sosah' charm-offensive / dialect-hurdle story — PIR-16 minor-party narrative carrier.
mkini author: 'Tackling the iceberg' (top story).
Incumbent state govt. 36 candidates in NS. Defending 20 seats (incl. all 11 DAP seats). Aminuddin as 'poster boy' MB candidate.
BN: 25 candidates in NS under BN-PN understanding (PAS/Gerakan/Wawasan/MIPP fill remaining 11). 'Tok mat' Rantau, Jalaluddin Pertang retained.
BN component. Wee Ka Siong 'not a merger' + MCA Youth sec-gen Saw Yee Fung told to stay out of NS. Internal dissent over PN cooperation. Chennah: Siow Kong Choon vs Anthony Loke.
BN lead component. Tok Mat (Rantau), Jalaluddin (Pertang), Johari, Zahid as national figures. Zool Amali Hussein = former Seremban Umno Youth chief (defector to Bersatu).

PH component. Defending all 11 NS seats (won PRU-14 2018 + PRN 2023). Under pressure post-Sabah/Johor defeats. Quit Melaka state govt. Loke as sec-gen faces leadership referendum. UEC + 41 kampung baru Cina issues.
PH component. Aminuddin (Linggi), Dr Azizul Hakim Mahdi (Rantau vs Tok Mat), Siti Aishah Seman (Gemas).
PH component. Zarinna Abu Zarin (Lenggeng), Ahmad Shahir Mohd Shah (Paroi), Nasir Rahman (Bagan Pinang). In Melaka: Adly still seated with govt while 4 DAP reps moved to opposition.

entities.context: PN component (within BN-PN understanding). 'PN-BN understanding boosts PAS hopes in NS polls' (NST). Hadi: 'PN to assist BN during NS polls campaign'. PAS candidates: Dr P. Kumar (Lobak), Kamarol Ridzuan Mohd Zain (Paroi), Abdul Fatah Zakaria (Bagan Pinang).
PN component. Lee Boon Shian (Bukit Kepayang vs DAP Nicole Tan).
PN component. Ridzuan Ahmad (Gemas — Bersatu-quit figure), Razali Abu Samah (Sikamat). PIR-06 defector-destination party.
PN component (Malaysian Indian People's Party). Sathes Kumar Nilameham (Lukut vs DAP Choo Ken Hwa).
Zamani Ibrahim (Nilai N.10, 5-cornered).
Dayana Dal (Jeram Padang, 4-cornered).
T. Tharmaseelan (Rahang, 4-cornered). 'Sosah' charm-offensive dialect narrative (mkini).

entities.context: Sat out NS. Tony Pua claims Bersama was 'spoiler' that cost PH seats in Johor. PIR-16 spoiler/vote-split narrative reference.
Harapan Youth wing. Demands BN ministers/deputy ministers quit Cabinet over PN alliance — 'BN is ungrateful despite trust and positions given by PM.' Day-1 PIR-16 amplification of BN-PN backlash.

Marquee straight fight: Anthony Loke (PH-DAP sec-gen/Transport Minister) vs Siow Kong Choon (BN-MCA). DAP sec-gen defends. PIR-07 marquee + PIR-16 Loke leadership-referendum seat.
Defends Chennah. Marquee PIR-07 candidate. (See PIR-16 entry for full narrative context.)
MCA candidate vs Anthony Loke in Chennah straight fight.

entities.context: Marquee straight fight. FMT full text (18 Jul): Tok Mat (Mohamad Hasan, Umno dep president / FM / ex-MB 2004-2018 / 5 terms since 2004; 2023 maj 10,280) vs PH Dr Azizul Hakim Mahdi. Mixed seat, Rembau parliamentary. Demographics: Malay 54.8%, Indian 27.6%, Chinese 16%. Zahid accompanied nomination. Day-1: Tok Mat 'act now or lose chance forever' at Sendayan Air Base Family Day.
PKR candidate vs Tok Mat in Rantau straight fight.
Bersatu candidate in Linggi (N.32) 3-cornered vs Aminuddin (PH) + Faizal Ramli (BN).
PH candidate in Sikamat (N.13); Aminuddin's political secretary. Tests PH without MB on ballot.
Bersatu information chief; candidate in Sikamat (N.13) 3-cornered. PIR-06 Tier-4 seat candidate.
Wawasan candidate in Sikamat (N.13) 3-cornered.
3-cornered: BN Datuk Mohd Asna Amin vs Amanah Zarinna Abu Zarin vs Bersatu Zool Amali Hussein (former Seremban Umno Youth chief — defector). Shifting alliances.
BN candidate in Lenggeng 3-cornered.
Amanah candidate in Lenggeng 3-cornered.
Bersatu candidate in Lenggeng; former Seremban Umno Youth chief — defector. PIR-16 shifting-alliances carrier.
DAP incumbent in Nilai (N.10) 5-cornered vote-split test.

Independent candidate in Sri Tanjung (N.33) 5-cornered.
Straight fight: PKR Siti Aishah Seman vs Wawasan Ridzuan Ahmad (Bersatu-quit figure). PIR-06 'keluar' signal anchored here.
PKR candidate in Gemas straight fight vs Ridzuan Ahmad (Wawasan, Bersatu-quit).
Straight fight: DAP incumbent Teo Kok Seong vs MCA Chong Fui Ming. BN hopes regain ground. (May overlap director-list 'N.20 Bembang' — unconfirmed.)
DAP incumbent in Bahau straight fight vs MCA's Chong Fui Ming.
MCA candidate in Bahau straight fight vs DAP incumbent Teo Kok Seong.

3-cornered BN stronghold. FMT confirms: BN Suhaimi Aini (incumbent; 2023 maj 135 votes — RAZOR MARGIN, highest-risk marginal; never left Umno since 1959) vs PH Allif Ibrahim vs Bersatu Akmal Noradzmi Abd Rahim.
BN incumbent in Kota (BN stronghold), 3-cornered.
PH candidate in Kota 3-cornered.
Bersatu candidate in Kota 3-cornered.
Urban straight fight: DAP Chew Seh Yong vs PAS Dr P. Kumar.
DAP candidate in Lobak urban straight fight vs PAS' Dr P. Kumar.
PAS candidate in Lobak urban straight fight vs DAP's Chew Seh Yong.
Urban straight fight: DAP incumbent Nicole Tan Lee Koon vs Gerakan Lee Boon Shian.
DAP incumbent in Bukit Kepayang urban straight fight vs Gerakan's Lee Boon Shian.
Gerakan candidate in Bukit Kepayang vs DAP incumbent Nicole Tan.

Urban seat. CORRECTION/CLARIFICATION: FMT Tok Mat article (18 Jul) reports 3-CORNERED: PAS/PN Kamarul Ridzuan Mohd Zain (incumbent, 2023 maj 5,539) vs PH Ahmad Shahir Mohd Shah vs Bersatu's Nazree Yunus. (Malay Mail nomination lineup described Paroi as straight fight Amanah vs PAS — discrepancy; FMT is more detailed/recent. 3-cornered retained as lead.)
Amanah candidate in Paroi urban straight fight vs PAS' Kamarol Ridzuan.
PAS candidate in Paroi urban straight fight vs Amanah's Ahmad Shahir.
Coastal/Port Dickson straight fight: DAP incumbent Choo Ken Hwa vs MIPP Sathes Kumar Nilameham.
DAP incumbent in Lukut coastal straight fight vs MIPP's Sathes Kumar Nilameham.
MIPP candidate in Lukut vs DAP incumbent Choo Ken Hwa.
Coastal/Port Dickson straight fight: Amanah Nasir Rahman vs PAS Abdul Fatah Zakaria. Early test of support in PD parliamentary constituency.

DAP incumbent in Repah straight fight vs MCA's Koh Kim Swee.

BN bigwig Jalaluddin Alias retains seat. CLARIFICATION via FMT (18 Jul): 3-WAY contest — Jalaluddin (BN, seeking 3rd term, touted as potential MB) vs PH's Umry Abdul Khois vs Bersatu's Faizal Fadli Idrus. Falls under Jelebu parliamentary constituency (Jalaluddin MP since 2018).

PH Abd Latif A Tambi (70, oldest candidate).

entities.source_url: https://www.malaysiakini.com (gnews: Royal dispute: Rembau's disputed chieftain sacks 3 nobles)
entities.context: NS royal/adat dispute site — disputed chieftain sacked 3 nobles; ousted noble argues council cannot hold meetings due to court injunction. PIR-16 adat dispute peripheral seat.

entities.entity: Tan Sri Ahmad Badri Mohd. Zahir
entities.context: New Felda chairman (replaced Ahmad Shabery Cheek, effective 30 Jun). Campaign-adjacent peripheral figure. Ex-Treasury secretary-general (2018-2020).
Former Felda chairman (tenure ended 30 Jun). Peripheral campaign-adjacent figure.

entities.source_url: https://www.nst.com.my (gnews: Kiandee asks Muhyiddin if Bersatu Supreme Council still has quorum - NST Online)
entities.context: CRITICAL NEW precursor escalation (NST headline-intel, 19 Jul, cycle 064654). Kiandee escalated from 'PN has grounds (asas kukuh) to remove Bersatu' to questioning whether Bersatu's Supreme Council even has quorum — implies mass exits have hollowed out the decision-making body. Director-flagged 'termination'-adjacent signal (questions organisational functionality). Threshold for formal PN-MT removal still NOT crossed; this is the strongest pre-threshold signal this cycle. Watch for Bersatu Supreme Council formal response.

entities.entity: Muhyiddin new coalition after state election
entities.context: CRITICAL PIR-06 PN-exit signal — FULL TEXT via FMT (19 Jul 09:00 MYT): 'Muhyiddin said the party will form a new coalition after the state election, hinting at a PN exit.' Strongest pre-threshold signal — escalate monitoring for post-NS-PRN formal announcement. Corroborates 'Bersatu exit from PN imminent?' (mkini SNAPSHOT 18 Jul 18:00). Not yet a formal expulsion/withdrawal → threshold NOT crossed, but approaches it.

entities.entity: Hadi denies PAS made PN toxic, blames Bersatu
entities.context: CRITICAL director-flagged 'toxic PN' entity — FULL TEXT via FMT (18 Jul 12:48 MYT). Hadi BLAMES Bersatu: 'rift caused by Bersatu's misconduct within PN.' Counter to Muhyiddin's accusation that PAS made 'baseless allegations and defamatory claims' to sideline Bersatu. Muhyiddin cited Perlis crisis (MB post PAS→Bersatu). Rift stated at Jempol nomination (Serting, Palong, Jeram Padang, Bahau). PAS severed political ties with Bersatu June 8; both remain in PN. Claim/counter-claim = strongest internal-rupture discourse signal.

PIR-06+PIR-16 narrative. Hadi (18 Jul): PN-BN ties 'lebih daripada berkahwin' (more than marriage); coalitions will assist each other in NS campaign. Formal ties 'dimuktamadkan dan dinilai dari semasa ke semasa' (finalised and evaluated from time to time). Analyst Zaharuddin Sani: 'bukan sekadar retorik' — frames as market test for a new coalition.

entities.entity: PAS severed political ties with Bersatu (June 8)
entities.context: PIR-06 operational fact — FULL TEXT via FMT. PAS severed political ties with Bersatu on June 8; both parties remain in PN and used same logo for Johor polls. Underpins the operational-split narrative.

entities.entity: Bersatu advised to quit PN with Gerakan, MIPP
entities.context: PIR-06 analyst recommendation — FULL TEXT via FMT (19 Jul 09:00 MYT). IIUM's Lau Zhe Wei advises Bersatu to leave PN now (no longer gains from staying, PAS fallout worsening) and take Gerakan + MIPP — would strip PN of multiethnic image. 'Bersatu should at least drag Gerakan with them.' Corroborates Muhyiddin new-coalition hint.

entities.entity: Bersatu heading for wipeout in NS
entities.context: PIR-06 analyst consensus — FULL TEXT via FMT. USM's Azeem Fazwan Ahmad Farouk expects Bersatu wiped out in NS: 'Bersatu is in no position to win seats going solo.' Azmil Tayeb (USM): Bersatu hasn't distinguished itself from Umno/PAS; 'all that people see coming out of Bersatu is the infighting.' PN fragmented image deals blow to PAS too.

PIR-06 coalition configuration confirmation — Awani full text (16 Jul 23:12 MYT). PN chairman Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar announced remaining PN candidates at Kompleks PAS NS. Resolves 'mystery of 11 BN seats.' Confirms BN 25 / PN 11, no clashes. PN candidates: Danni Rais (Klawang), Mohamad Rafie Ab Malek (Ampangan), Kumar Paramasevam (Lobak), Razali Abu Samah (Sikamat), Erik Michael (Mambau), Lee Boon Shian (Bukit Kepayang), Sathes Kumar Nilameham (Lukut); 4 incumbents retained: Mohd Fairuz Mohd Isa (Serting), Kamarol Ridzuan Mohd Zain (Paroi), Abdul Fatah Zakaria (Bagan Pinang), Ridzuan Ahmad (Gemas).

entities.entity: BN ops rooms open to assist PN (Gemas machinery-sharing)
entities.context: PIR-06 machinery-sharing CONFIRMED at seat level — NST full text (19 Jul 11:59 MYT). Tampin Umno chief Mohd Isam Mohd Isa: ALL BN operations rooms in Gemas will remain open to assist PN (Wawasan) candidate's campaign. 'We do not want any sabotage.' Aim: win all 3 state seats in Tampin parliamentary. Explicit: 'merely an understanding to face the election, not a political merger.' BN-MCA/MIC relationships 'remain intact.'

entities.entity: Zahid: NS performance determines future alliance
entities.context: PIR-06+PIR-16 narrative — FULL TEXT via FMT (18 Jul). Zahid: BN will assess NS performance before deciding wider PN cooperation; results decide whether 'understanding' extends to Melaka polls and GE16. 'Politics is very dynamic.' Learned from Johor geopolitics. NS = test for PRU16 alliance.

Tampin Umno division chief / Tampin MP. Carrier of seat-level machinery-sharing confirmation: all BN ops rooms in Gemas open to assist PN (Wawasan) candidate. 'Not a political merger'; BN-MCA/MIC relationships intact. Urged no racial/religious sentiment exploitation in Tampin.

PIR-06+PIR-16 grassroots-acceptance narrative — FMT BM full text (19 Jul). BN/PN grassroots in Jempol welcome cooperation, expect cross-voting in non-contested seats, hope it ends 'perpecahan' (division). Supporters: Mohamad Yusof Abdullah (PN, 78), Noorizan Karim (BN, 49), Jefri Baharom (PN, 60), Mohd Zuki (BN, 61), Abu Ubaidah (PN, 38). 'Penyatuan ummah inilah yang diharapkan oleh akar umbi Umno.'

IIUM (International Islamic University Malaysia) analyst. PIR-06: advises Bersatu to quit PN with Gerakan+MIPP (strip PN of multiethnic image). PIR-16: MCA benefits from BN-PAS alliance (PAS vote transfer to MCA); MCA faces choice — chase scarce Chinese votes or opt for BN-PAS cooperation and win seats. 'Second option may be better for MCA.' Johor MCA doubled seats to 8 with PAS votes.

Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) analyst. Expects Bersatu wiped out in NS: 'Bersatu is in no position to win seats going solo.' Bersatu lacks strong political base, unlikely to recover while internal tensions unresolved.

Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) analyst. Bersatu problems run deeper than PAS issues; hasn't distinguished itself from Umno/PAS despite competing for same Malay electorate. 'All that people see coming out of Bersatu is the infighting.' PN fragmented image deals blow to PAS too.

Analyst, Global Asia Consulting. PRIORITY PIR-16 carrier of 'NS = makmal politik (political laboratory)' framing. Hadi 'more than marriage' = 'bukan sekadar retorik' — forms perception that PN-BN cooperation already exists though not formally sealed. 'The most important question is not whether the bride and groom agree, but whether the children accept the new marriage' (children = grassroots). Political marriage without family blessing is fragile.

Felo Majlis Profesor Negara (National Professorial Council Fellow). PIR-16 'Johor formula' carrier. Hadi's statement reflects increased cooperation tested unofficially in Johor. Johor PAS-Umno success motivates clearer NS understanding. BN not contesting all 36 + PAS only contesting BN-uncontested seats = 'persefahaman begitu nyata.' Model not necessarily applicable to all states — Johor/NS/Melaka landscapes differ.

Sunway University analyst. PIR-16: PH weakness incentivised BN-PAS pact. 'If PH collapses in Negeri Sembilan, BN and PN would collaborate further in GE16.' MCA weaker in NS than Johor. Non-Malays unwilling to stake future on MCA if perceive risk of PH defeated by BN-PAS alliance. DAP/supporters concerned about BN-PAS ramifications.

PRIORITY PIR-07 — PH incumbent in N.28 Klawang (director-listed seat). Day-1 campaign (Awani full text, 19 Jul 12:11 MYT): campaigned at Pasar Minggu Kuala Klawang (Jelebu) same time as cousin/PN rival Danni Rais — met peacefully, joked, no provocation. Took down flags after Majlis Daerah Jelebu warning. 'No special challenge… campaign healthily, follow rules.' Mature-campaign framing carrier.

PRIORITY PIR-07 — PN candidate in N.28 Klawang (director-listed seat). Confirmed via Awani teka-teki (16 Jul 23:12) and Day-1 campaign article (19 Jul 12:11 MYT). Cousin of PH incumbent Bakri Sawir — 'two cousins face off' marquee Day-1 storyline. (Spelling variant 'Danii Rais' in teka-teki article.)
PRIORITY PIR-07 — Bersatu candidate in N.28 Klawang (director-listed seat), 3-cornered vs Bakri Sawir (PH) + Danni Rais (PN). Awani full text (19 Jul 12:11 MYT).

PRIORITY PIR-07 — BN candidate in N.27 Chembong (director-listed seat), NOW RECOVERED via FMT Tok Mat article (18 Jul). Has represented Chembong since 2008; retained seat with 4,335-vote majority in 2023. Straight fight vs PH's Danish Nazran Murad.
PRIORITY PIR-07 — PH candidate in N.27 Chembong (director-listed seat), NOW RECOVERED via FMT (18 Jul). Straight fight vs BN's Zaifulbahri Idris.

PN (PAS) candidate in Lobak. Awani teka-teki (16 Jul 23:12) names 'Kumar Paramasevam' for Lobak — likely the full name of the 'Dr P. Kumar' listed in Malay Mail nomination lineup (PAS candidate in Lobak urban straight fight vs DAP's Chew Seh Yong). Name-clarification entity.
PN candidate in Mambau. Awani teka-teki (16 Jul 23:12). Mambau = one of the 3 seats (Lobak, Mambau, Lukut) Loke said MCA 'surrendered' to PN ('MCA biggest loser').

PN incumbent in Serting (retained). Awani teka-teki (16 Jul 23:12). One of 4 PN incumbents retained (others: Kamarol Ridzuan Paroi, Abdul Fatah Zakaria Bagan Pinang, Ridzuan Ahmad Gemas). Serting = one of the Jempol-district seats Hadi nomination covered.

Bersatu candidate in Paroi. FMT Tok Mat article (18 Jul) reports Paroi as 3-CORNERED: PAS/PN Kamarul Ridzuan Mohd Zain (incumbent, 2023 maj 5,539) vs PH Ahmad Shahir Shah vs Bersatu Nazree Yunus. (Malay Mail had described Paroi as straight fight — discrepancy; FMT more detailed.)
PH candidate in Pertang (3-way vs BN's Jalaluddin Alias + Bersatu's Faizal Fadli Idrus). FMT full text (18 Jul).
Bersatu candidate in Pertang (3-way vs BN's Jalaluddin Alias + PH's Umry Abdul Khois). FMT full text (18 Jul).

Seat ceded to PN (Loke: MCA 'surrendered' Mambau to PN). Previously held by senior MCA figures. PN candidate Erik Michael (Awani teka-teki). Part of the 'MCA biggest loser' narrative cluster (Lobak, Mambau, Lukut).
PN incumbent seat (Mohd Fairuz Mohd Isa, retained). One of the Jempol-district seats (Serting, Palong, Jeram Padang, Bahau) where Hadi spoke at nomination and stated the PAS-Bersatu rift.

Parliamentary constituency (3 state seats). BN aiming to win all 3 via BN-PN understanding. Tampin Umno chief Mohd Isam Mohd Isa confirmed machinery-sharing: BN ops rooms in Gemas open to assist PN candidate. Johari met Tampin BN election machinery (NST Johor-formula article).
Deputy Prime Minister. Anwar conveyed the 'resign if you want to attack unity partners' reminder in discussions yesterday with DPMs Zahid + Fadillah, following NS election campaign.
Communications Minister. Present at Anwar's Ali Baba Bujang Lapok unity speech at National Month launch (Ipoh, Sultan Azlan Shah Health Ministry Training Institute, 19 Jul 12:21 MYT).
Perak Menteri Besar. Present at Anwar's Ali Baba Bujang Lapok unity speech (Ipoh, 19 Jul). Perak unity govt ties unaffected by Melaka DAP pullout (per prior-cycle Star headline).

entities.entity: NS = makmal politik (political laboratory for PRU16)
entities.context: PRIORITY PIR-16 framing — NEW this cycle. Zaharuddin Sani (Global Asia Consulting): NS 'sedang dijadikan makmal politik' (being made a political laboratory). 'Jika eksperimen ini meningkatkan sokongan, ia boleh berkembang menjadi model kerjasama menjelang PRU16. Jika gagal, kedua-dua pihak masih mempunyai ruang untuk berkata bahawa mereka tidak pernah benar-benar berkahwin.' Frames every NS seat as a PRU16 indicator. Azmi Hassan: Johor PAS-Umno success is the motivator. Corroborates Zahid 'NS performance determines future alliance' + Johari 'Johor formula' + Utusan Johari 'PRN NS penentu kerjasama BN-PN PRU16.'

PIR-16 narrative. Johari Abdul Ghani (Umno VP): adapt BN's successful Johor election formula to NS — 3 core elements: party strength, strategic candidate selection, election machinery capability. 'We cannot assume the same formula will apply automatically.' Spoke at Tampin BN machinery meeting. Azmi Hassan: Johor PAS-Umno success motivates NS understanding; 'pengorbanan PAS mungkin dikatakan berbaloi.'

entities.entity: Act now or lose chance forever (Tok Mat)
entities.context: PRIORITY PIR-16 existential Malay-future framing — FULL TEXT via NST (19 Jul 14:46 MYT, FRESH). Tok Mat at Sendayan Air Base Family Day Carnival: BN-PN understanding 'important to safeguard the future of race and religion.' First mooted 2018 but 'fell through halfway.' 'If we don't do it this time, we will miss the boat… our children and grandchildren will become mere traders in their own country.' Frames BN-PN as EXISTENTIAL for Malay future. Directly counters DAP/Madani multi-ethnic narrative.

PRIORITY PIR-16 narrative — FULL TEXT via NST (19 Jul 14:03 MYT, FRESH). Hadi (FB post): support PAS+Umno+MCA+MIC+DHPP 'non-extreme' formula; extend to Sabah/Sarawak to 'save Malaysia.' Reject 'unnatural Malay groups with DAP' (secular/chauvinist). 'Malay Muslims must lead because they dominate the Muslim population.' NS: BN 25 / PN 11 / PAS 5 under PN. Frames NS as PRU16 realignment test. Expanded 'penyatuan undi Melayu' with conditional multiracial extension.

entities.entity: Reject unnatural Malay groups with DAP
entities.context: PIR-16 narrative (Hadi, NST 19 Jul 14:03 MYT). Reject 'unnatural Malay groups that are with DAP, namely fanatical secular and extreme chauvinist groups that threaten Islam and the Malay community.' Implicit attack on PH/Umno-DAP federal unity cooperation. Frames DAP as existential threat to Malay/Islamic interests.

entities.entity: Ali Baba Bujang Lapok / 40 thieves were also united (Anwar)
entities.context: PRIORITY PIR-16 COUNTER-NARRATIVE — FULL TEXT via NST (19 Jul 12:21 MYT). Anwar at National Month launch (Ipoh): 'Unity alone is not enough… the 40 thieves were also united.' 'Unite for what? We must unite to improve the quality of life of the people. Not unite to steal or plunder timber and wealth.' Multi-ethnic independence narrative; implicit counter to BN-PN 'Malay unity' framing + Tok Mat existential framing. Fahmi + Perak MB Saarani present.

PRIORITY PIR-16 DISCIPLINE narrative — FULL TEXT via NST (19 Jul 14:47 MYT, FRESH). Anwar warns ministers/deputy ministers/agency heads to RESIGN if they intend to use federal positions to attack unity-govt partners during state campaigns. Federal-aligned parties free to contest each other in state polls, but office-holders must keep discipline. Conveyed in discussions with DPMs Zahid + Fadillah. (Article marked 'MORE TO COME'.) Federal-state firewall.

entities.entity: BN to decide NS MB candidate only if it wins (Jalaluddin)
entities.context: PIR-16 'MB after PRN' confirmation — FULL TEXT via FMT (18 Jul). Jalaluddin Alias (state Umno chief, touted as potential MB): 'Once we achieve victory, BN's leadership will decide who is best suited to be appointed the menteri besar.' 'Several leaders capable.' Jalaluddin seeking 3rd term in Pertang (3-way vs PH Umry + Bersatu Faizal Fadli).

entities.entity: BN-PN cooperation only to avoid vote splits, not alliance (Wee)
entities.context: PIR-16 narrative — FULL TEXT via FMT (18 Jul). Wee Ka Siong (MCA president): cooperation 'does not amount to an alliance'; each party upholds ideological principles + checks and balances. 'Not an alliance and it will not lead to the formation of a new party or coalition.' Decision finalised early hours of July 15. MCA involvement = ensure Chinese community/multiracial/vernacular education/religious freedom representation. Hadi: PAS agreed in principle to let BN defend 14 prior seats + PAS contest 3 it won.

entities.entity: BN-PAS tie-up will force MCA difficult choice
entities.context: PIR-16 narrative — FULL TEXT via FMT (19 Jul). MCA must choose between winning elections and losing core supporters. Lau Zhe Wei: MCA benefits from BN-PAS alliance (PAS vote transfer — Johor MCA doubled to 8 seats) but may discourage MCA supporters. 'Second option (BN-PAS cooperation) may be better for MCA.' Wong Chin Huat: MCA weaker in NS than Johor; non-Malays won't stake future on MCA if perceive PH defeat risk.

PRIORITY PIR-16 viral-amplifier opinion — FULL TEXT via FMT (19 Jul, 7009c). 'By quietly opening the side door for PAS in NS, Umno is committing the oldest form of political miscalculation: validating the very force designed to replace it.' Frames BN-PN as 'friends with benefits'; PAS transfers ideological brand to BN candidates. Cites Amanat Hadi (1981, Umno supporters as 'infidels'). 'Religious populism does not seek to share power; they seek to replace the decadent old order entirely.' Warns urban non-Malay alienation (Seremban, Rasah, Nilai). 'The tiger is always hungry, and it does not care for understandings.'

entities.entity: Lim rejects Islamophobia claim, Hadi coined green wave
entities.context: PIR-16 counter-narrative — FULL TEXT via FMT (18 Jul). Lim Lip Eng (Kepong MP, DAP): Hadi himself coined 'gelombang hijau' (green wave) in 2018 (Harakah) re PAS gains in Perak + Terengganu in GE14 — 4 years before GE15. 'PAS cannot use religion as an armour against political criticism.' Cites PAS deputy president Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man: 'PAS never claimed to be sole embodiment of Islam.' Counters Hadi's Islamophobia claim.

PIR-16 narrative. Term 'green wave' (gelombang hijau) — Hadi coined 2018 (PAS mouthpiece Harakah) re GE14 PAS gains in Perak + Terengganu. Hadi now says DAP used term after GE15 to stigmatise PAS/Muslims (Islamophobia claim). Lim Lip Eng counters with 2018 provenance. Active framing contest.

PIR-16 historical reference. 1981 Amanat Hadi labelled Umno supporters as 'infidels' — cited in FMT 'Umno's dangerous dance with PAS' opinion as the historical frame for why Umno-PAS cooperation is risky (Umno spent half a century framing PAS as extremist). Now Umno 'inviting back the very force it spent half a century containing.'

PIR-16 counter-narrative — FULL TEXT via FMT Muhyiddin (18 Jul 16:52 MYT). Accuses Umno/BN of deflecting blame while in government + using 'ummah unity'/'Malay unity' calls to win Malay voters. 'This is just a charade to deceive the eyes of the Malays and the people in order to gain power.' Claims Bersatu 'free of all this charade' + committed to anti-kleptocracy/1MDB founding struggle. Direct counter to Tok Mat/Hadi 'Malay unity' framing.

PIR-16 PROCESS narrative — Awani full text (19 Jul 13:58 MYT). Rembau voters want mature, courteous campaign focused on people's needs/economy, not provocation. Voters quoted: Malek Ibrahim (77, retiree), Mohd Firdaus Nordin (35, Imam Masjid Kampung Pedas Tengah), Siti Hawa Yahya (56, trader — cost of living). 'Rakyat sekarang sudah cerdik.' Bakri Sawir (Klawang) embodies it. 103 candidates / 36 seats confirmed.

entities.entity: Johari: adapt Johor formula for NS
entities.context: PIR-16 narrative — NST full text (19 Jul 11:34 MYT). Johari Abdul Ghani (Umno VP) at Tampin BN machinery meeting: adapt Johor election formula (party strength + candidate selection + machinery) to NS local realities. 'Cannot assume same formula will apply automatically.' BN victory in NS = part of broader state-level rebuild. (See also 'Johor formula' entity.)

Kepong MP (DAP), four-term. Rejects Hadi's Islamophobia claim; says Hadi coined 'green wave' term in 2018 (Harakah). 'PAS cannot use religion as an armour against political criticism.' Cites Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man contradicting Hadi.
PAS deputy president. Cited by Lim Lip Eng: 'PAS had never claimed to be the sole embodiment of Islam' — used to counter Hadi's Islamophobia claim / framing of PAS criticism as attack on Islam.

entities.source_url: https://www.nst.com.my (gnews: Ab Rauf says PH only mirrored federal unity in Melaka - NST Online)
entities.context: Melaka Chief Minister. PIR-16 Melaka DAP withdrawal narrative peripheral figure. Headline-intel (NST, 14 Jul 10:08 GMT): 'Ab Rauf says PH only mirrored federal unity in Melaka' — frames Melaka state govt arrangement as mirroring federal unity govt. Full text not recovered (gnews JS-render). Context for Melaka DAP quit-state-govt narrative.

entities.source_url: https://www.malaymail.com (gnews: Melaka assembly: Four DAP assemblymen moved to opposition but Amanah's Adly still seated with govt)
entities.context: Amanah's Melaka leader. In Melaka DAP withdrawal: 4 DAP assemblymen moved to opposition bloc but Adly still seated with govt — Amanah did not follow DAP out. Highlights PH-component divergence in Melaka. Frames DAP isolation vs Amanah compliance. (Per prior-cycle Melaka DAP narrative; now added as distinct entity.)
